facts: The heart pumps blood through blood vessels to deliver oxygen to all cells, not just to the lungs.
When you breathe faster during exercise, your lungs take in more oxygen so your blood can carry more oxygen to your working muscles.
Carbon dioxide is a waste gas that your cells produce; the blood carries it to the lungs, where you breathe it out, not back into the body.
The lungs and heart work together: the lungs add oxygen to the blood, and then the heart pumps that oxygen-rich blood to the rest of the body.
misconceptions: Students believe that the heart pumps air around the body because they feel it beating in their chest and associate it with breathing.
Students think that oxygen travels directly from the lungs to the cells without any help from the heart or blood vessels.
